subject: computer-science
description: Software & Hardware MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: What are the physical components of a computer system called?
mcqs.explanation: The physical components of a computer system are called hardware — they are the parts you can see and touch.

mcqs.q: A collection of programs designed for a specific need is called:
mcqs.explanation: A collection of programs designed for a specific need is called software.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is hardware?
mcqs.explanation: Keyboard is hardware — a physical input device. MS Word, OS and Antivirus are all software.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is software?
mcqs.explanation: An Operating System is software. Mouse, CPU and Printer are hardware.

mcqs.q: Software can be divided into how many main types (application, system, general purpose)?
mcqs.explanation: Software can be divided into three types — application, system and general purpose software.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is NOT considered hardware?
mcqs.explanation: Operating System is software — not hardware. Keyboard, RAM and CPU are hardware components.

mcqs.q: Where in Windows do you find the 'Add new hardware' option?
mcqs.explanation: The 'Add new hardware' option is found in the Control Panel.

mcqs.q: A hard disk drive (HDD) is what type of storage?
mcqs.explanation: A hard disk drive is a non-volatile data storage device — it retains its data even when power is off.

mcqs.q: A set of instructions that directs a computer's hardware to perform a task is called:
mcqs.explanation: A set of instructions that directs a computer's hardware to perform a task is called a programme or software programme.

mcqs.q: What is the conventional designation for pre-released software?
mcqs.explanation: Beta is the conventional designation for pre-released software — it follows the alpha stage and precedes the final release.

mcqs.q: A device driver is:
options: A software programme that controls a particular type of hardware device
mcqs.explanation: A device driver is a software programme that controls a particular type of hardware device attached to a computer.

mcqs.q: What are the two main types of software?
mcqs.explanation: The two main types of software are system software and application software.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is part of system software?
options: Device drivers, OS, compilers, disk formatters, text editors, utilities
mcqs.explanation: System software includes device drivers, operating systems, compilers, disk formatters, text editors and utilities — all of which help the computer operate efficiently.

mcqs.q: What is the main role of system software?
options: Controlling, integrating and managing the individual hardware components
mcqs.explanation: System software is responsible for controlling, integrating and managing the individual hardware components of a computer system.

mcqs.q: Application software is used to:
options: Accomplish specific tasks other than running the computer system
mcqs.explanation: Application software is used to accomplish specific tasks other than just running the computer system — e.g. an image viewer, a browser, a word processor.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is NOT an application software?
mcqs.explanation: Windows XP is NOT application software — it is an operating system (system software). MS Word, image viewer and web browser are application software.

mcqs.q: Which type of software is used for storing, manipulating and managing data in a database environment?
mcqs.explanation: A Database Management Software (DBMS) is used for storing, manipulating and managing data — including format, names of fields, record and file structures.

mcqs.q: Which type of software contains rows and columns?
mcqs.explanation: Spreadsheet software contains rows and columns and is used for calculations — e.g. MS Excel, Lotus 1-2-3, Quattro Pro.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is graphic software used for?
mcqs.explanation: Graphic software is used to draw pictures, 3D images and animation — e.g. Adobe Photoshop, CorelDRAW.

mcqs.q: Multimedia software is used to:
options: Create visual presentations with audio and video clips
mcqs.explanation: Multimedia software is used to create visual presentations with the help of audio and video clips.

mcqs.q: Which of the following are examples of multimedia software?
mcqs.explanation: Adobe Flash and MS PowerPoint are examples of multimedia software.

mcqs.q: Windows Media Player is an example of:
mcqs.explanation: Windows Media Player is entertainment software — designed for leisure and media playback.

mcqs.q: Which of the following are spreadsheet software?
options: MS Excel, Lotus 1-2-3, Corel Quattro Pro
mcqs.explanation: MS Excel, Lotus 1-2-3 and Corel's Quattro Pro are examples of spreadsheet software.

mcqs.q: Which is the biggest computer software company in the world?
mcqs.explanation: Microsoft is the biggest computer software company in the world.

mcqs.q: A virtual printer is:
options: Computer software whose user interface and API resembles that of a printer driver
mcqs.explanation: A virtual printer is computer software whose user interface and API resembles that of a printer driver — e.g. PDF Creator.

mcqs.q: Reference software emulates and expands upon print reference forms such as:
options: Dictionary, translation dictionary, encyclopedia, thesaurus and atlas
mcqs.explanation: Reference software emulates and expands upon print reference forms including dictionary, translation dictionary, encyclopedia, thesaurus and atlas.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is an example of reference software?
mcqs.explanation: Encyclopedia is an example of reference software.

mcqs.q: A computer virus is best described as:
options: A malicious software programme loaded onto a user's computer without their knowledge
mcqs.explanation: A computer virus is a malicious software programme loaded onto a user's computer without their knowledge that performs malicious actions.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is system software?
mcqs.explanation: An Operating system is system software. MS Word, MS Excel and MS PowerPoint are application software.

mcqs.q: Examples of computer hardware include:
options: Keyboard, Mouse, Printer, CPU and RAM
mcqs.explanation: Examples of computer hardware include Keyboard, Mouse, Printer, CPU and RAM — all physical components.

mcqs.q: Input devices, processing unit, output devices and auxiliary storage devices are collectively called:
mcqs.explanation: Input devices, processing unit, output devices and auxiliary storage devices are all classified as hardware.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is a hardware company of computers?
mcqs.explanation: Dell is a hardware company of computers. Microsoft, Adobe and Oracle are primarily software companies.

mcqs.q: Which is the largest hardware company of computers?
mcqs.explanation: HP (Hewlett-Packard) is the largest hardware company of computers.

mcqs.q: What can hardware be best described as?
options: Physical parts of the computer that we can see and touch
mcqs.explanation: Hardware refers to the physical parts of the computer that we can see and touch.

mcqs.q: A hard disk drive is classified as:
mcqs.explanation: A hard disk drive is a non-volatile data storage device.

mcqs.q: How many main types of Machine Learning are there?
mcqs.explanation: There are three main types of Machine Learning: Supervised Learning, Unsupervised Learning and Reinforcement Learning.

mcqs.q: Image classification and identity fraud detection are typical applications of:
mcqs.explanation: Image classification and identity fraud detection are typical applications of Supervised Learning.

mcqs.q: Customer segmentation and clustering are applications of:
mcqs.explanation: Customer segmentation and clustering are key applications of Unsupervised Learning.

mcqs.q: Game AI, robot navigation and real-time decisions are applications of:
mcqs.explanation: Game AI, robot navigation and real-time decisions are typical applications of Reinforcement Learning.

mcqs.q: Recommender Systems are an application of which type of ML?
mcqs.explanation: Recommender Systems are an application of Unsupervised Learning — finding patterns and groupings without labelled data.

mcqs.q: Weather forecasting and market forecasting are typically:
mcqs.explanation: Weather forecasting and market forecasting are typical Supervised Learning tasks (regression problems).

mcqs.q: Big Data Visualisation, Dimensionality Reduction and Meaningful Compression are applications of:
mcqs.explanation: Big Data Visualisation, Dimensionality Reduction and Meaningful Compression are applications of Unsupervised Learning.

mcqs.q: Skill acquisition and learning tasks (where an agent improves over time) are applications of:
mcqs.explanation: Skill acquisition and learning tasks where an agent improves over time are applications of Reinforcement Learning.

mcqs.q: Diagnostics, Customer Retention and Estimating Life Expectancy are typical applications of:
mcqs.explanation: Diagnostics, Customer Retention and Estimating Life Expectancy are typical Supervised Learning applications.
